steps.title: Write the equation using point-slope form
steps.content:
We have a point $(16, 0)$ (the x-intercept) and slope $m = \frac{3}{4}$. Apply the point-slope form:
$$y - 0 = \frac{3}{4}(x - 16)$$

steps.title: Simplify to slope-intercept form to find the y-intercept
steps.content:
Expand and simplify:
$$y = \frac{3}{4}x - \frac{3}{4} \cdot 16$$
$$y = \frac{3}{4}x - 12$$
In slope-intercept form, the y-intercept is the constant term: $-12$.
